articles.headline: Mixing Up Your Workouts May Lower Your Risk of Early Death
articles.body: Harvard researchers analyzed data from over 111,000 adults across 30-plus years and found that people who regularly engaged in a variety of physical activities had a lower risk of premature death, even when total exercise volume was the same. Walking, lifting, gardening, racquet sports — the mix matters. Benefits appeared to plateau around 20 weekly MET hours, suggesting more isn't always more. The takeaway: don't just do more. Do different.
articles.takeaway: Add one new activity type to your weekly routine. Swap a second run for a strength session or a bike ride.
articles.source: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health / BMJ Medicine

articles.headline: Staying Mentally Active Throughout Life Could Delay Alzheimer's by 5 Years
articles.body: A study published in Neurology tracked nearly 2,000 adults over eight years and found that those with the highest lifetime cognitive enrichment had a 38% lower risk of developing Alzheimer's disease. Activities like reading, writing, and learning languages at multiple stages of life were linked to slower cognitive decline. High enrichers developed Alzheimer's about five years later than low enrichers. This isn't just for retirees — the mental habits you build now count.
articles.takeaway: Pick up a book, learn something new, or subscribe to a challenging podcast. Your future brain is shaped by what you do today.

product.name: Outlive - Peter Attia Audiobook
product.blurb: The definitive book on living longer and better. Required reading for anyone who trains.

articles.headline: Your Brain Health Is Being Built Right Now, Not Just in Old Age
articles.body: A new American Heart Association scientific statement published in the journal Stroke says brain health isn't just about what happens later in life. Sleep quality, mental health, diet, social connection, and environment all shape how your brain functions and ages — starting early. The statement points to the AHA's Life's Essential 8 as a practical guide and highlights Mediterranean-style eating and healthy sleep as especially protective. Small daily habits add up over decades.
articles.takeaway: Treat sleep, stress management, and diet as brain investments — not just body investments. The payoff is decades away, but it starts now.

articles.headline: Even 2 Minutes of Hard Effort Can Improve Long-Term Health
articles.body: A Washington Post column citing recent research says you don't need long workouts to get longevity benefits from intensity. Adding even brief bursts of vigorous movement to a routine that already includes walking can meaningfully improve fitness and long-term health outcomes. The piece highlights that getting older doesn't mean slowing down — and that short doses of high-intensity effort can transform health without requiring a huge time commitment.
articles.takeaway: Tack 2 to 5 minutes of hard effort onto your next easy walk or run. Stair sprints, hill surges, or fast intervals all count.

product.name: HOKA Clifton 9
product.blurb: Max cushion for easy days and long runs. Keeps your legs fresh for hard sessions.

quote.text: The mind is the athlete. The body is simply the means it uses.
quote.author: Bryce Courtenay
quote.context: Australian author known for exploring human resilience and the power of mental determination in his novels.

workout.title: Tuesday Tempo Builder
workout.warmup: Start with 8 minutes at an easy, conversational pace — you should be able to talk in full sentences. Finish the warmup with 4 short 20-second pickups at a slightly faster effort to wake up your legs.

exercises.name: Easy Base Run
exercises.notes: Effort level: easy. This is your warmup mileage — keep it relaxed, nose breathing if possible.

exercises.name: Tempo Intervals
exercises.notes: Effort level: moderate-hard. Comfortably uncomfortable — you can speak a few words but not hold a conversation. Recover with an easy jog or walk during the rest.

exercises.name: Easy Float
exercises.notes: Effort level: easy. Between your last tempo and cooldown — shake out your legs and bring your heart rate down naturally.

workout.cooldown: Walk or jog very easily for 5 minutes. Finish with 2 minutes of standing quad, calf, and hip flexor stretches — hold each for 30 seconds per side.

product.name: Saucony Endorphin Speed 5
product.blurb: The shoe Chris trains and races in. Fast, light, built for people who take running seriously.

meal.title: Turkey and Egg White Breakfast Scramble with Spinach and Feta
ingredients: salt, pepper, and garlic powder to taste
meal.instructions: 1. Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook 2 minutes until soft. 2. Add ground turkey, breaking it up, and cook until no longer pink — about 4 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. 3. Add spinach and cherry tomatoes, stir until spinach wilts. 4. Pour in egg whites and the whole egg, scramble gently until just set. Top with feta and serve immediately, with toast if you want extra carbs.
meal.tip: Batch-cook the ground turkey the night before so this comes together in under 5 minutes in the morning — just reheat and add the eggs.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
